items.d: The subject where you read and write stories from Britain.
items.s: We have ___ and read stories from Britain.
items.hintDe: Die Sprache, die du gerade lernst.

items.d: The subject you study to talk to people in France.
items.s: My sister studies ___ so she can talk to people in France.
items.hintDe: Die Sprache, die man in Paris spricht.

items.d: The subject where you play the guitar.
items.s: We play the guitar in our ___ lesson.
items.hintDe: In diesem Fach spielst du Gitarre.

items.d: The subject where you work with numbers.
items.s: I like ___ because I am good with numbers.
items.hintDe: In diesem Fach rechnest du mit Zahlen.

items.d: The subject about countries, cities and rivers.
items.s: In ___ we read about countries, cities and rivers.
items.hintDe: In diesem Fach geht es um Länder und Flüsse.

items.d: The subject about light, space and the Earth.
items.s: We find out about light and space in ___.
items.hintDe: In diesem Fach lernst du über Licht und den Weltraum.

items.d: The subject where you run, jump and play outside.
items.s: In ___ we run, jump and play outside.
items.hintDe: In diesem Fach läufst du und machst Sport.
items.note: verify-fix: Turnen promoted to full (everyday Austrian term)

items.d: The subject where you paint and make pictures.
items.s: I painted a picture of my dog in ___.
items.hintDe: In diesem Fach malst du Bilder.

items.d: The school subject about kings, queens and famous people from the past.
items.s: In ___ we read about famous people from the past.
items.hintDe: Das Schulfach, in dem du über Könige, Königinnen und die Vergangenheit lernst.
items.note: v1 carrier used out-of-bank 'Romans'/'learned'; rephrased with in-bank 'famous people from the past'.

items.d: The school subject where you work on computer games and webpages.
items.s: In ___ we play computer games and make a webpage.
items.hintDe: Das Schulfach, in dem du am Computer arbeitest (kurz: IT).
items.note: Subject = IT. v1 carrier used 'laptops'/'use' (out of bank); rephrased with in-bank 'computer games' + 'webpage'.

items.d: The school subject where you make and paint a box or a car.
items.s: In ___ we make and paint a small box.
items.hintDe: Das Schulfach, in dem du mit deinen Händen Sachen baust und gestaltest (Werken).
items.note: Subject = DT. v1 'D&T' answer and 'built'/'tools' carrier were out of bank; rephrased with in-bank 'make and paint a small box'. Closest distractor is art.

items.d: Very happy about a good thing.
items.s: I was ___ to stay at home with my family.
items.hintDe: Du fühlst dich so, wenn etwas Schönes passiert.
items.sbRef: SB Unit 1 'A SONG 4 U' / Word file: 'I was glad to stay at home.'
items.note: Carrier from the unit song + master-list example ('I was glad to stay at home.'). Definition avoids 'happy' as headword-free; 'happy' offered as partial answer/translation.

items.d: How long a road is. A road can be one, two or many of them.
items.s: My new school is one ___ from the park.
items.hintDe: Damit misst du, wie weit ein Ort entfernt ist (1000 Meter).
items.sbRef: SB Word file: "My school's one kilometre from my house."
items.note: Carrier from master-list example, reworked to keep 'house' (out of bank) out -> 'from the park'. 'metre' is not in the bank so distractors are in-bank concrete nouns. -er spelling marked partial (British -re is the taught form).

items.d: To not go out, but to be in with your family all day.
items.s: It is cold, so we want to ___ today.
items.hintDe: Du gehst nicht weg, sondern bleibst den ganzen Tag daheim.
items.sbRef: SB Word file: 'Are you going to stay at home?'
items.note: Whole phrase goes in the blank (carrier reworded from master-list example) so 'home' is never exposed alone. Distractors are in-bank verb phrases.

items.d: To go to new places, often on a plane or a train.
items.s: My family and I ___ to many countries every summer.
items.hintDe: Du machst eine weite Reise von einem Ort zu einem anderen.
items.sbRef: SB Word file / song: 'Did you travel far?'
items.note: Master-list example 'Did you travel far?' has out-of-bank 'far', so carrier reworded to a present-simple frame (blank takes 'travel'/'travels'). Past 'travelled' fits the definition but not this carrier's present frame -> partial.

items.d: At once, just after one thing happens.
items.s: I have breakfast ___ I get up in the morning.
items.hintDe: Eine Sache passiert sofort, gleich nachdem die andere passiert (sobald).
items.sbRef: SB Word file: 'I do my homework as soon as I get home.' / Grace's text: 'As soon as I get to school, I sweep my classroom.'
items.note: verify-fix: after is defensible→partial; distractors clearly wrong

items.d: To put your clothes on.
items.s: I wash, ___ and have breakfast with my mum and dad.
items.hintDe: Du machst es morgens, wenn du in deine Kleidung schlüpfst.
items.sbRef: SB p.9 (Michael's day)
items.note: Carrier from Michael's text (SB p.9): 'I wash, get dressed and have breakfast'. Present-tense carrier, so 'got dressed' is partial.

items.d: To spend time outside on your feet, often with your dog.
items.s: After school, I ___ with my dog.
items.hintDe: Du gehst nach draußen und gehst gemütlich herum, oft mit dem Hund.
items.sbRef: SB p.9 (Michael's day)
items.note: From Michael's text 'I go for a quick walk with my dog' (SB p.9), 'quick' dropped (out of bank). Present-tense carrier, so 'went for a walk' is partial.

items.d: A time at school when you study one subject.
items.s: Our first ___ begins in the morning, at seven o'clock.
items.hintDe: So heißt eine einzelne Schulstunde, zum Beispiel von sieben bis acht Uhr.
items.sbRef: SB p.9 (Grace's day)
items.note: From Grace's text 'Our first lesson starts at 7.30 a.m.' (SB p.9); 'starts/7.30' reworded to in-bank words. Singular only in this carrier; 'lessons' accepted from the definition.

items.d: To make food before you eat it.
items.s: I help my mum to ___ food for our supper.
items.hintDe: Du machst etwas fertig, zum Beispiel das Essen vor dem Abendessen.
items.sbRef: SB p.9 (Grace's day)
items.note: verify-fix: cook/make are synonyms→partial; distractors no longer synonyms

items.d: To wear clothes like a jacket or a hat.
items.s: After breakfast, I ___ my school clothes.
items.hintDe: Du nimmst Kleidung und ziehst sie an, zum Beispiel deine Jacke.
items.sbRef: SB p.9 (Grace's day)
items.note: From Grace's 'put on my school uniform' (SB p.9); 'uniform' is out of bank, reworded to 'school clothes'. 'put on' covers present and past, so one full answer. 'to wear' deliberately kept out of the distractor pool (too close in meaning).

items.d: The food you eat in the evening.
items.s: In the evening we eat ___.
items.hintDe: So heißt die Mahlzeit, die du am Abend isst.
items.note: verify-fix: dinner is an accepted partial, removed from pool

items.d: Happening every day.
items.s: I get up at seven o'clock every day. It is my ___ life.
items.hintDe: Etwas, das jeden Tag passiert, ist ___.
items.note: Masterlist sense ('My daily routine ...'); reworded because 'routine' is out of bank. 'daily routine' as a phrase recurs in the SB headings. hintDe nudges meaning, not the answer.

items.d: A thing that shows you all the days and months of a year.
items.s: It shows all the days of the year. It is like a big ___.
items.hintDe: Darauf siehst du alle Tage, Wochen und Monate eines Jahres.
items.sbRef: SB p.10 (Snake adventures)
items.note: From the story 'It's like a big calendar.' (SB p.10), with an in-bank lead-in for clarity. 'timetable' is a strong same-class distractor (both show days).

items.d: the mother of your mum or dad
items.s: My ___ lives in Mexico.
items.hintDe: Das ist die Mama von deiner Mama oder deinem Papa.
items.sbRef: SB Unit 1, story p.10 ("My dad, my mum, my grandmother and I") / MORE Words and Phrases: "My grandmother lives in Mexico."

items.d: a short story you tell to make people laugh
items.s: We all laughed at Tom's good ___.
items.hintDe: Etwas Lustiges, das du erzählst, damit andere lachen.
items.sbRef: SB Unit 1, story p.11: "It wasn't a joke!" (carrier reworded so the answer is unambiguous)
items.note: Carrier invented because the verbatim SB line "It wasn't a joke." is too open as a cloze (song/lie/dream also fit).

items.d: It makes you scared.
items.s: The story was very ___ and I was scared.
items.hintDe: Etwas, das dir Angst macht.

items.d: the time of year after the cold months and before summer
items.s: My birthday is in ___, in May.
items.hintDe: Die Jahreszeit nach dem Winter, wenn es wärmer wird.
items.sbRef: SB Unit 1, story p.11: "In the spring and summer, the shadows ... look like a huge snake." (carrier reworded; SB sentence has untaught words snake/steps)
items.note: "spring" = the season; the carrier anchors it with "in May" so the season reading is unambiguous against the distractor "summer".

items.d: a place in a town or city
items.s: There is a lot of rubbish in this ___ of the town.
items.hintDe: Ein Teil von einer Stadt oder einem Ort, wo Menschen leben.

items.d: to pay for a place or ticket before you go
items.s: You can ___ your holiday today.
items.hintDe: Einen Platz oder ein Ticket vorher zahlen, damit er für dich frei ist (nicht das Buch zum Lesen).
items.sbRef: SB Unit 1, leaflet p.11: "Contact us to book today!" + MORE Words and Phrases: "You can book your holiday today."
items.note: "to book" = reserve/pay in advance, NOT the noun "book" (Buch). Carrier "book your holiday" forces the verb reading; hintDe spells out the contrast.

items.d: that many people like very much
items.s: Maths is a ___ subject at our school.
items.hintDe: Etwas, das viele Menschen gerne mögen.
items.sbRef: SB Unit 1, leaflet p.11: "This is a very popular tourist attraction in Mexico." (carrier reworded; SB phrase uses untaught tourist/attraction)

items.d: a dark place on the wall behind a thing in the light
items.s: Look — there is a dark ___ behind me on the wall.
items.hintDe: Die dunkle Stelle, die entsteht, wenn etwas vor dem Licht steht.
items.sbRef: SB Unit 1, story/leaflet p.10-11: "You can see a shadow move down the side of the pyramid steps." (carrier reworded; SB sentence uses untaught snake/steps/pyramid)
items.note: Story-context word; carrier keeps the dark/light idea without the untaught snake/pyramid vocabulary.

items.d: To go to a place or to people and be there for a time.
items.s: In the holidays we ___ our grandmother in Mexico.
items.hintDe: Du fährst irgendwohin, um dir einen Ort oder Menschen anzusehen.
items.sbRef: SB Unit 1, leaflet (p.11): "A lot of tourists visit the Mayan city of Chichen Itza."; masterlist exampleSb

items.d: A thing with red, purple, white and many more in it.
items.s: There are many ___ parrots at the zoo.
items.hintDe: Etwas, das ganz viele Farben hat.
items.sbRef: masterlist exampleSb: "There are many colourful parrots at the zoo."

items.d: From one end of a thing to the end of it.
items.s: We go ___ the river to the park.
items.hintDe: Du gehst an etwas der Länge nach vorbei, z.B. an einem Fluss.
items.sbRef: masterlist exampleSb: "Snakes slide along the sand."; SB poem (p.13): "...and slide along the ground."
items.note: Carrier avoids 'snake' (out of bank) but keeps the textbook's along-a-line sense.

items.d: To go like a lizard, near the road and under stones.
items.s: Lizards ___ across the road and under the stones.
items.hintDe: Du bewegst dich ganz tief am Boden, so wie eine Eidechse.
items.sbRef: masterlist exampleSb: "Snakes crawl around and into stones."; SB poem (p.13): "I crawl around the rocks and stones."
items.note: Carrier uses 'lizards' (in bank) for the low-to-the-ground sense; 'snake' is out of bank.

items.d: To sit down and be still for a short time when you are tired.
items.s: After a long day at school, I ___ before I do my homework.
items.hintDe: Du setzt dich hin und machst kurz nichts, weil du müde bist.
items.sbRef: masterlist exampleSb: "I take a rest after school."; SB poem (p.13): "...and then I take a rest."
items.note: Carrier subject 'I' makes only 'take a rest' grammatical (full); 3sg/past forms are partial.

items.d: Maths, art, music and history are all kinds of these.
items.s: English is my favourite ___.
items.hintDe: Englisch, Mathe oder Kunst ist jeweils so etwas in der Schule.
items.sbRef: masterlist exampleSb: "English is my favourite subject."; SB 14: "... is my favourite subject."
items.note: V-8 fix: dropped "at school" (school is a (school)-subject form token)

items.d: A short time to play and eat after a lesson at school.
items.s: In the ___ we go outside and play.
items.hintDe: Die kurze Zeit zwischen den Stunden, in der du spielst und isst.

items.d: It shows you what lessons you have on every day of the week.
items.s: She has four English lessons in her ___.
items.hintDe: Der Plan, der dir zeigt, wann du welche Stunden hast.
items.sbRef: masterlist exampleSb: "She has four English lessons in her timetable."; SB 13: "Here is his timetable."

items.d: It is on the road, but cars do not go on it.
items.s: There is a new ___ in my town now.
items.hintDe: Hier fährst du mit dem Rad, nicht die Autos.
items.note: verify-fix: plural breaks "a new ___" agreement→partial

items.d: A man who can make the rules.
items.s: A ___ can make the rules.
items.hintDe: Ein Mann, der ein Land regiert.
items.sbRef: SB p.15 (FIND OUT: Democracy) — "A king, queen or person can make the rules."
items.note: Carrier verbatim from SB p.15 Democracy task; also matches master list "A king can make the rules."

items.d: Making a lot of noise.
items.s: I do not like ___ people.
items.hintDe: Etwas oder jemand, der viel Lärm macht.
items.sbRef: SB p.15 (Our local world) — "noisy people"
items.note: Carrier from master list "I don't like noisy people."; word also appears on SB p.15 problem list.

items.d: A woman who can make the rules.
items.s: A ___ can make the rules.
items.hintDe: Eine Frau, die ein Land regiert.
items.sbRef: SB p.15 (FIND OUT: Democracy) — "A king, queen or person can make the rules."
items.note: Carrier verbatim from SB p.15 Democracy task; also matches master list "A queen can make the rules."

items.d: Food, bottles and boxes that you do not want any more.
items.s: There is too much ___ in the street.
items.hintDe: Das wirfst du weg, zum Beispiel altes Essen.
items.sbRef: SB p.15 (Our local world) — "rubbish in the street"
items.note: verify-fix: definition was wrong (rubbish=Müll, not food only)

items.d: How to be careful with a tablet or mobile phone.
items.s: Our teacher talked about ___ in class today.
items.hintDe: Wie du vorsichtig bist, wenn du im Internet bist.
items.note: Master-list sense relates to a website; carrier rephrased to taught-only words (master list 'helpful tips ... website' has untaught words). Compound kept whole; definition uses taught 'tablet'/'mobile phone' to avoid untaught 'internet'/'computer'.

items.d: What you think about a thing.
items.s: In my ___, this is the best book.
items.hintDe: Das, was du über eine Sache denkst.
items.note: Master list "I always say my opinion" uses untaught 'say'; carrier rephrased to the natural 'In my opinion, ...' frame with taught-only words.

items.d: You read it on a tablet or mobile phone.
items.s: Read the ___ on your tablet.
items.hintDe: So eine Seite liest du auf dem Tablet oder Handy.
items.note: Master list "Go online and read the webpage" uses untaught 'online'; carrier rephrased to taught-only words. Definition avoids untaught 'page'/'internet'/'computer' via taught 'tablet'/'mobile phone'.
